hypothesis: Extending the paper's finding that reactor yield optimization converges at intermediate bicarbonate concentrations (~1.5 M) rather than maximum concentrations, and that KOH concentration showed surprisingly limited influence on reactor yield despite its known role in cathodic microenvironment modulation, a closed-loop Bayesian optimization campaign targeting a composite reactor yield metric that incorporates long-term electrode stability (measured as the fractional decay in JCO over 60-minute electrolysis runs) will identify optimal KOH concentration regimes that are systematically different from those identified by the 5-minute steady-state reactor yield optimization, because KOH-mediated catalyst flooding and carbonate precipitation at the gas-diffusion electrode surface—processes invisible at short timescales—will emerge as dominant performance-limiting variables at extended operation, causing the KOH concentration parameter to shift from low to high SHAP importance when the optimization objective explicitly penalizes temporal degradation.
